channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-07-03
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. In the scienceverse/metacheck project, Lakens has made foundational additions to the `data_check` branch, laying the groundwork for new datacheck modules. Meanwhile, the giladfeldman/docpluck project has resolved several issues, including the "efendic" problem, and shipped 7 fixes, while also improving rendering and fixing issues with titles, sections, and glyphs. Interestingly, the removal of the sign-up feature in the JamieCummins/regcheck project on the `staging` branch may indicate a shift in focus towards existing users. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across these open-source research tools. As these projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing how they will intersect and support one another in the future.

51 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: statcheck

Most active:

docpluck19 commits
regcheck18 commits
escicheck6 commits
metacheck4 commits
statcheck2 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `data_check` branch was the focus of the recent work, with additions made by Lakens. Specifically, Lakens made "additions" to the branch, although the details of these additions are not specified. Additionally, Lakens created the "first version of datacheck modules" on the `data_check` branch, laying the groundwork for this new functionality. The `data_check` branch is where all the changes took place, with no other branches mentioned in the commits. The work appears to be foundational, setting the stage for further development of the datacheck modules.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 342b25c [data_check] additions — Lakens
  • bd8c890 [data_check] first version of datacheck modules — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: statcheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
MicheleNuijten/statcheck

The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch was worked on, where "admin updates" were made, as stated in the commit message. The branch `feature-non-apa-newer` was then merged from the repository https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck into itself, as indicated by the merge commit. No specific features, fixes, or modules were described in the commit messages beyond "admin updates". The `feature-non-apa-newer` branch is the only branch mentioned in the provided commit messages. No other branches or changes were mentioned in the given commits. The changes were made by MicheleNuijten.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 50ab5e6 [feature-non-apa-newer] Merge branch 'feature-non-apa-newer' of https://github.com/MicheleNuijten/statcheck into feature-non-apa-newer — MicheleNuijten
  • ffba7eb [feature-non-apa-newer] admin updates — MicheleNuijten

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 8 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where documentation was updated to reflect the outcome of a 2026-07-03 run, with "efendic RESOLVED, ip_feldman C3 deferred" as noted in the commit message "docs(triage): record 2026-07-03 run outcome — efendic RESOLVED, ip_feldman C3 deferred". A handoff document was also iterated upon, as indicated by the commit "docs(handoff): 2026-07-03 iterate run — efendic RESOLVED, 7 fixes shipped, PARTIAL", which shipped 7 fixes. The rendering of body author-affiliation footnotes was fixed by stripping the footnote block, as stated in the commit "fix(render): strip body author-affiliation footnote block (efendic) + v2.4.107". Additionally, fixes were made to the title, sections, and glyphs, including dropping the Elsevier CrossMark "T" widget from the H1 title, promoting Sage/APA back-matter headings, and recovering specific glyphs in table cells, such as "×-as-3" and "2-for-minus", as described in commits like "fix(title): drop Elsevier CrossMark 'T' widget from the H1 title + v2.4.106" and "fix(glyph): recover ×-as-3 in interaction-term table cells (efendic) + v2.4.103".

8 commit(s) today:

  • 216eb10 [main] docs(handoff): 2026-07-03 iterate run — efendic RESOLVED, 7 fixes shipped, PARTIAL — Gilad Feldman
  • ff3a223 [main] docs(triage): record 2026-07-03 run outcome — efendic RESOLVED, ip_feldman C3 deferred — Gilad Feldman
  • c44d203 [main] fix(render): strip body author-affiliation footnote block (efendic) + v2.4.107 — Gilad Feldman
  • 8cad252 [main] fix(title): drop Elsevier CrossMark "T" widget from the H1 title + v2.4.106 — Gilad Feldman
  • 34585f5 [main] fix(sections): promote Sage/APA back-matter headings via taxonomy + v2.4.105 — Gilad Feldman
  • 4c0c3e9 [main] fix(glyph): stop dropped-minus recovery sign-flipping SE table cells (efendic) + v2.4.104 — Gilad Feldman
  • 4e5c3ff [main] fix(glyph): recover ×-as-3 in interaction-term table cells (efendic) + v2.4.103 — Gilad Feldman
  • 54b7923 [main] fix(glyph): recover 2-for-minus in Camelot HTML table cells (efendic B-column) + v2.4.102 — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 2 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the "dev" and "main" branches. On both branches, the commit "fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker" was made, which specifically re-enabled Fable 5 in the model picker. This change was applied to both the development and main branches, as evidenced by the identical commit messages on both the "dev" and "main" branches. The re-enabling of Fable 5 in the model picker was the sole focus of these commits. The commits were made by Davidvandijcke on the same day, July 2, 2026.

2 commit(s) today:

  • d93b6f8 [dev] fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker — Davidvandijcke
  • d6df84a [main] fix(web): re-enable Fable 5 in the model picker — Davidvandijcke
  • 05aad76 [main] Merge pull request #245 from Davidvandijcke/dev — David Van Dijcke
  • 49a72b3 [dev] Merge remote-tracking branch 'origin/main' into sync/reconcile-v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 2e86c5f [dev] fix(i18n): localize confirmation email + review page follows review language (#244) — David Van Dijcke
  • 907c629 [dev] Merge pull request #242 from Davidvandijcke/release/v1.8.0 — David Van Dijcke
  • f54b584 [dev] fix(deploy): enable RLS on schema_migrations bookkeeping table (#243) — David Van Dijcke
  • ba19e15 [dev] chore(release): v1.8.0 — Davidvandijcke
  • 262305d [dev] docs(experiments): record PR-F language experiment (keep Design A) (#241) — David Van Dijcke
  • 2e1c796 [dev] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) (#240) — David Van Dijcke
  • ef4f2eb [feat/i18n-setup-compare] feat(i18n): localize setup + compare pages; follow site language (#239) — Davidvandijcke
  • e8c3845 [dev] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) (#238) — David Van Dijcke
  • f1fbfe9 [dev] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) (#237) — David Van Dijcke
  • c3370c4 [feat/237-lang-eval] feat(i18n): cross-language review-consistency eval harness (PR-F) (#236) — Davidvandijcke
  • 228b694 [feat/nl-flemish] i18n(nl): Dutch site-UI wording refinements (#235) — Davidvandijcke
  • 1910472 [dev] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) (#234) — David Van Dijcke
  • 9ac6256 [feat/i18n-status-review-pages] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) — Davidvandijcke
  • 8faa90a [dev] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232) — David Van Dijcke
  • 395a58c [feat/231-site-i18n] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) — Davidvandijcke
  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with three commits made to it. The first commit, made on 2026-07-03, "sync from escicheckapp@ec9eb9f (effectcheck v0.6.13)" as stated by Gilad Feldman, synchronized the repository with effectcheck version 0.6.13. Another commit on 2026-07-01, "sync from escicheckapp@edb3c7b (effectcheck v0.6.12)", synchronized the repository with effectcheck version 0.6.12, as noted in issue #25. A third commit, also on 2026-07-01, "sync from escicheckapp@928a5bc (effectcheck v0.6.11)", synchronized the repository with effectcheck version 0.6.11, referenced in issue #24.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 7ec3c89 [main] sync from escicheckapp@ec9eb9f (effectcheck v0.6.13) (#26) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
